title: Hamburger Ketchup Macaroni with Peas
description: The classic sweet-and-savory ketchup skillet many families grew up with. One pot, pantry ingredients, and dinner's on the table.
instructions: Brown the ground beef in a large skillet or Dutch oven. Drain excess grease if needed.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 3 to 5 minutes.
Stir in the ketchup, butter, salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Add the beef broth (or water) and bring to a simmer.
Stir in the uncooked macaroni, cover, and cook until the pasta is tender, about 10 to 12 minutes, stirring occasionally and adding a splash of water if it gets too thick.
Stir in the frozen peas during the last 3 to 5 minutes so they heat through without becoming mushy.
Taste and add a little more ketchup, salt, or pepper if desired.
notes: A splash of Worcestershire sauce adds a richer flavor.
Serve with buttered bread or saltine crackers.
This has the classic sweet-and-savory ketchup flavor that many families grew up with.
leftovers: Leftovers reheat well with a splash of water. Keeps 3 days in the refrigerator.
storage: Cool, then store covered in the refrigerator. Reheat gently on the stove with a splash of water or broth.
whyWeChoseThis: One pot, start to finish — easy for Papaw and easy to clean up.
Everything comes from the pantry and freezer.

editorialNotes.note: Added to the cookbook — the sweet-and-savory skillet Papaw grew up on.
